action.reasoning: Observation: mean_r2 improved to -23k (new best) but still driven by r2_ptend_q~-53k (val mean_r2~0.66 with q~0.3); other components stable/strong (t~0.58, rad~0.93, precip~0.74). Large gen gap on q-tendency persists despite noise/WD/dropout/weighted loss/best-on-val_r2. Training curve shows steady val improvement but test q explodes. Analysis: overfitting to val-year-8 q-dynamics (dist shift to test); capacity (2048/4) too high for robust q-preds, output init allows large errors, q_weight=20 too aggressive. Curves confirm val_mse~0.0038 plateau. Decision: reduce capacity (hidden=1024, blocks=6), stronger reg (dropout=0.3, noise=0.05, WD=1e-3, output_std=1e-5), lower q_weight=5, start_lr=5e-4 + scheduler, epochs=30 (follows <20-epoch rule after prior long runs), clip=1.0; update model/train.py (keep predict.py); train then submit_eval on best.pt to target positive q-r2 and mean_r2.
